s2.ptxt: Apply a light coat of spindle oil or gasoline to new O-rings, and install them to each injector.
Apply a light coat of spindle oil or gasoline where the fuel delivery pipe contacts the O-ring.
Push the fuel injector while turning it to install the injector in the fuel delivery pipe.
Position the fuel injector connector outward.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to twist the O-ring.
After installing the fuel injector, check that it turns smoothly. If not, reinstall it with a new O-ring.

s2.ptxt: Install 6 new insulators to the intake manifold.
Place the fuel delivery pipe which has the 6 fuel injectors installed to it in position on the intake manifold.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to drop the fuel injectors when installing the fuel delivery pipe.

s2.ptxt: Temporarily install the 5 bolts which are used to hold the fuel delivery pipe to the intake manifold.

atten3.ptxt: After installing the fuel injectors, check that they turns smoothly. If not, reinstall the injectors with new O-rings.

s2.ptxt: Tighten the 5 bolts which are used to hold the fuel delivery pipe to the intake manifold.
Connect the 6 fuel injector connectors.
Push in the tube connector onto the pipe until the tube connector makes a "click" sound.

item.ptxt: Before connecting the tube, make sure that it is not damaged. Make sure that there is no dirt present on the connecting surfaces.
After connecting, check that the fuel tube connector and the pipe are securely connected by pulling on them.

s2.ptxt: Install the No. 2 fuel pipe clamp.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL INTAKE AIR SURGE TANK ASSEMBLY

atten3.ptxt: DO NOT apply oil to the bolts listed below:

entry.ptxt: No. 1 Surge Tank Stay and Surge Tank
Throttle Body Bracket and Surge Tank

s2.ptxt: Install a new gasket to the intake air surge tank.
Using a 5 mm hexagon socket wrench, install the 4 bolts.
Install the intake air surge tank with the 2 nuts and 2 bolts.
Connect the connector.
Install the vacuum hose clamp with the bolt.
Connect the union to check valve hose.
Connect the No. 1 ventilation hose.
Install the clamp and connect the throttle with motor body assembly connector.
Connect the vapor feed hose.
Connect the 2 water by-pass hoses to the throttle with motor body assembly.
Install the air cleaner cap sub-assembly, and connect the 3 bands.
Connect the mass air flow meter connector (*1).
Connect the No. 2 ventilation hose (*2).
Connect the hose band (*3).
Connect the 3 vacuum hoses.
Close the radiator drain cock plug and 2 cylinder block drain cock plugs.

torqueitem.ptxt: for cylinder block drain cock plug

s2.ptxt: Slowly fill the radiator with TOYOTA Super Long Life Coolant (SLLC).

item.ptxt: TOYOTA vehicles are filled with TOYOTA SLLC at the factory. In order to avoid damage to the engine cooling system and other technical problems, only use TOYOTA SLLC or similar high quality ethylene glycol based non-silicate, non-amine, non-nitrite, non-borate coolant with long-life hybrid organic acid technology (coolant with long-life hybrid organic acid technology consists of a combination of low phosphates and organic acids).
Contact your TOYOTA dealer for further details.

s2.ptxt: Slowly pour coolant into the radiator reservoir tank until it reaches the FULL line.
Press the inlet and outlet radiator hoses several times by hand, and then check the level of the coolant.
If the coolant level is low, add coolant.
Install the radiator cap sub-assembly and reservoir tank cap.
Start the engine, and warm it up.

atten4.ptxt: Adjust the air conditioner set temperature to MAX (HOT).

s2.ptxt: Stop the engine, and wait until the engine coolant cools down.
Add engine coolant to the FULL line on the radiator reservoir.

s-1.ptxt: CONNECT CABLE TO NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL

atten2.ptxt: Do not remove the radiator cap while the engine and radiator are still hot. Pressurized, hot engine coolant and steam may be released and cause serious burns.

atten3.ptxt: Before performing each inspection, turn the A/C switch OFF.

s2.ptxt: Fill the radiator with coolant and attach a radiator cap tester.
Warm up the engine.
Using a radiator cap tester, increase the pressure inside the radiator to 118 kPa (1.2 kgf*cm, 17 psi), and check that the pressure does not drop.
If the pressure drops, check the hoses, radiator and water pump for leaks. If no external leaks are found, check the heater core, cylinder block and cylinder head.
Check fuel pump operation.

s3.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Turn the ignition switch on (IG) and push the intelligent tester main switch on.
Select the following menu items: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / ACTIVE TEST / FUEL PUMP / SPD.

Check for pressure in the fuel inlet tube from the fuel line. Check that sound of fuel flowing in the fuel tank can be heard. If no sound can be heard, check the integration relay, fuel pump, ECM and wiring connector.

s2.ptxt: Check for fuel leaks.

s3.ptxt: Check that there are no fuel leaks anywhere on the system after performing maintenance. If there is a fuel leak, repair or replace parts as necessary.

s2.ptxt: Turn the ignition switch off.
Disconnect the intelligent tester from the DLC3.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COWL TOP PANEL OUTER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the cowl top panel outer sub-assembly with the 4 bolts and 4 nuts.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL WINDSHIELD WIPER MOTOR AND LINK ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the windshield wiper motor and link assembly with the 4 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Tighten the bolts in the order shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Connect the connector.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL COWL TOP VENTILATOR LOUVER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Engage the 4 claws and install the cowl top ventilator louver sub-assembly.
Install the 2 clips.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL RH

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the front fender to cowl side seal RH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL LH

s2.ptxt: Engage the claw and install the front fender to cowl side seal LH.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Operate the wiper and stop the windshield wiper motor at the automatic stop position.
Clean the wiper arm serrations.
When reinstalling:

s3.ptxt: Clean the wiper pivot serrations with a wire brush.

s2.ptxt: Install the front wiper arm and blade assembly LH with the nut to the position shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Hold the arm hinge by hand to fasten the nut.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY RH

s2.ptxt: Operate the wiper and stop the windshield wiper motor at the automatic stop position.
Clean the wiper arm serrations.
When reinstalling:

s3.ptxt: Clean the wiper pivot serrations with a wire brush.

s2.ptxt: Install the front wiper arm and blade assembly RH with the nut to the position shown in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Hold the arm hinge by hand to fasten the nut.

s2.ptxt: Operate the front wipers while spraying washer fluid on the windshield glass. Make sure that the front wipers function properly and the wipers do not come into contact with the vehicle body.
Fit the 3 retainers and install the V-bank cover.
